concept: Chisel / Carving Tool / To Carve with Precision
description: A tool with a cutting edge for shaping wood, stone, or metal; to carve with precision. 'Kiru' names what reveals form—removing material to find shape within.
sound_symbolism: The word cuts clear. 'Ki' bites with sharp precision, edge entering. Then 'ru' carves the channel, form emerging from material.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kiru' (the chisel). As verb: 'mi kiru' (I chisel). Works with 'toka' (hammer) in carving work.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Detail and precision—the chisel creates the fine lines, the delicate relief, beauty in small scale.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Carving reveals—the chisel finds form hidden in wood and stone, subtractive art.
